The case involves alleged issues with cash sweep programs from 2022 through final court approval and is one of about 25 similar cases against financial institutions. The company will record a reserve for the full settlement in its first fiscal quarter of 2026, significantly affecting reported earnings for that period, even though the agreement was reached after quarter-end. * **$70 million settlement charge** will significantly depress Oppenheimer’s reported earnings for the first fiscal quarter of 2026, creating a notable one-time hit to profitability even though the cash payment is contingent on court approvals. **Oppenheimer Holdings** agreed to a $70 million class-action settlement over its cash sweep program, compared with plaintiff assertions of potential damages above $440 million.","score":0.94401723,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://openclassactions.com/settlements/oppenheimer-cash-sweep-class-action-settlement.php","title":"$70M Oppenheimer Cash Sweep Class Action Settlement","content":"Oppenheimer & Co.
